title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
statement_text: Every graph $H$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property, i.e., there exists $c>0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ contains a clique or stable set of cardinality at least $|G|^{c}$.
context_text: The Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is the central motivation of the paper. Despite extensive efforts it has only been proved for a small family of graphs. The paper extends it to pairs $\{H,\overline{J}\}$ where $H$ and $J$ are cycles, and more generally to graphs obtained by subdividing edges, using the framework of iterative sparsification.
paper_contribution: Proves that $\{H,\overline{J}\}$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property for any two cycles $H,J$, and more generally when $H$ and $J$ are obtained from smaller graphs by subdividing every edge exactly twice (or, for one of them, by subdividing every non-forest edge at least five times); this simultaneously extends the $C_5$ result and resolves the open question from [6] about longer cycles.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. III. Cycles and subdivisions
